news.headline: WGS slumps after hours as GeneDx forecasts lower full-year test volume
news.summary: GeneDx shares fell after hours after the company signaled softer full-year testing volume, reinforcing demand concerns first flagged at Q1. It deepened worries that the growth narrative that drove the stock to record highs in early June has cracked. This is the freshest negative operational catalyst ahead of the next quarterly report.

news.headline: WGS investor deadline: GeneDx investors with losses may lead the class action; August 3 deadline
news.summary: One of many law firms reminding shareholders of an August 3, 2026 lead-plaintiff deadline in the consolidated securities fraud litigation. The suits allege GeneDx misrepresented the performance and value of its Fabric Genomics acquisition ahead of a large impairment. The litigation remains a persistent overhang on the shares.

news.headline: GeneDx names Mark Gardner President to lead its next phase of scale and growth
news.summary: GeneDx appointed Mark Gardner as President (effective mid-June), reporting to CEO Katherine Stueland, to scale commercial and operations after rapid revenue growth. The addition comes amid the guidance reset and litigation, positioning new management to steer a turnaround. Investors are weighing whether it restores execution credibility.

news.headline: Carelon adds outpatient genome sequencing coverage, lifting insured coverage to ~80%
news.summary: Carelon, the largest U.S. lab benefit manager, added genome sequencing to its reimbursement policies, raising the share of insured patients with genome coverage to nearly 80%. It is a structural volume tailwind and arguably the most important positive catalyst of the period. It underpins the bull case that the sell-off is overdone.

news.headline: Hedge funder Eli Casdin is loading up on GeneDx shares
news.summary: Casdin Capital's Eli Casdin, a long-time GeneDx-linked investor, was reported buying shares into the post-crash weakness. Specialist accumulation is being read as a contrarian vote of confidence against the litigation-driven sell-off. It offers a bullish counterpoint to the heavy negative headline flow.

news.headline: GeneDx 49% drop triggers investor scrutiny over Fabric Genomics acquisition
news.summary: Start of a broad wave of class actions after a roughly 49% share-price crash and a large write-off (about 94% of the purchase price) on the Fabric Genomics acquisition, following a Q1 miss and guidance cut. Legal risk and reputational pressure weigh on the stock. This is the core catalyst behind the current drawdown.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings (est.)
catalysts.detail: Next quarterly report; the market is watching test volume, revised full-year guidance, and Fabric Genomics cleanup after the July preannouncement of softer volume.

catalysts.label: Warrant expiration (WGSWW)
catalysts.detail: Expiration of GeneDx warrants on July 22, 2026, a technical factor for capital structure and potential dilution.

catalysts.label: Class-action lead-plaintiff deadline
catalysts.detail: Deadline for shareholders to move to lead the consolidated securities fraud litigation tied to the Fabric Genomics impairment and guidance cut.

catalysts.label: New President execution
catalysts.detail: Mark Gardner joining as President; the market will watch whether new leadership stabilizes volume trends and rebuilds guidance credibility.

catalysts.label: Reimbursement coverage expansion
catalysts.detail: Further payer and Medicaid coverage decisions following the Carelon move could support genome/exome test volume growth.

pulse: GeneDx is under heavy pressure after a Q1 miss and a roughly 49% crash from an early-June record high near $52, driven by a large (~94%) write-off on the Fabric Genomics acquisition and a full-year guidance cut. That triggered a wave of securities class actions with an August 3 lead-plaintiff deadline, and a July 10 warning of lower full-year test volume added fresh downside. Against the negatives, structural positives remain: Carelon reimbursement lifted insured genome coverage to about 80%, the core testing business is still growing double digits, and specialist buyers like Eli Casdin have been accumulating. The appointment of Mark Gardner as President signals a push to stabilize execution. Setup is high-risk and headline-driven into the late-July/early-August Q2 print, which is the key swing between a washed-out bottom and further de-rating.
